title: Criminal Defence Strategy in Bangladesh: How to Fight Your Case and Win
metaTitle: Criminal Defence Strategy Bangladesh (2026): How to Fight & Win Your Case
metaDescription: Facing criminal charges in Bangladesh? Learn proven defence strategies — FIR quashing, bail, false case dismissal & trial tactics used by expert criminal lawyers in Dhaka.
keywords: how to fight criminal case Bangladesh
heroIntro: Being accused of a crime in Bangladesh — whether the charge is true or false — is one of the most stressful experiences a person can face. The legal system is complex, the police investigation process is aggressive, and the consequences of conviction can be life-altering. But an accusation is not a conviction. With the right criminal defence strategy, many cases are dismissed, acquitted, or resolved on favourable terms — if handled correctly from the very start.
toc: Why Criminal Defence Strategy Matters in Bangladesh
What to Do in the First 24 Hours After Arrest
Bail — Your First Line of Freedom
Understanding the FIR and Charges Against You
Challenging and Quashing a False FIR
Common Criminal Defence Mistakes to Avoid

sections.h2: Why Criminal Defence Strategy Matters in Bangladesh
sections.content:
In Bangladesh, the criminal justice system places the burden of proof on the prosecution — the state must prove guilt beyond reasonable doubt. A skilled defence does not need to prove innocence; it needs to create sufficient doubt about the prosecution's case.

Without an experienced criminal lawyer in Dhaka, accused persons often make self-incriminating statements to police, miss critical bail windows, fail to challenge inadmissible evidence, and do not adequately cross-examine unreliable witnesses. Each of these failures can turn a winnable case into a conviction.

sections.h2: What to Do in the First 24 Hours After Arrest
sections.content:
The first 24 hours after arrest are the most critical. Here is what you must do — and must not do:

Do: Contact a criminal lawyer immediately. Demand your constitutional right to be produced before a Magistrate within 24 hours (Article 33, Bangladesh Constitution). Identify the FIR sections cited against you.

Do NOT: Give any statement to police without your lawyer present. Sign any document without reading it carefully. Pay any informal payment to police — this is illegal and worsens your situation.

sections.h2: Bail — Your First Line of Freedom
sections.content:
Bail is the immediate priority in any criminal case. A person in custody cannot effectively participate in building their defence.

  • Bailable offences: Bail is a legal right — the Magistrate must grant it on first production.
  • Non-bailable offences: Bail is discretionary. Your bail lawyer argues health, family circumstances, weak evidence, and constitutional rights.
  • Anticipatory bail: If you fear arrest before it happens, apply immediately in Sessions Court or High Court. See: Anticipatory Bail Bangladesh Guide.

sections.h2: Understanding the FIR and Charges Against You
sections.content:
Your lawyer must obtain and analyse the FIR immediately to understand: who filed the complaint and their motive, which penal code sections are cited, whether the allegations are specific or vague, whether evidence exists or the case is word-of-mouth, and whether the FIR was filed with suspicious delay.

Many FIRs in Bangladesh contain vague and omnibus allegations designed to harass rather than prosecute. The Bangladesh High Court has a well-established line of authority holding that such FIRs should be quashed. Identifying this early is a core defence strategy.

sections.h2: Challenging and Quashing a False FIR
sections.content:
If the FIR is false, malicious, or legally defective, your lawyer files a quashing application before the High Court Division under Section 561A CrPC or via writ jurisdiction under Article 102 of the Constitution.

Courts quash FIRs where: the allegations are manifestly false, the FIR is vague and omnibus, the allegations are legally insufficient to constitute any offence, or continuing prosecution would be an abuse of process. See: How to Stop a False Criminal Case in Bangladesh.

Once bail is secured, defence preparation becomes the priority:

  • Gather evidence early. CCTV footage, call records, and bank statements are time-sensitive. Evidence that exists today may be unavailable in 6 months.
  • Identify favourable witnesses who can testify to your alibi, character, or contradictions in the prosecution's account.
  • Analyse the prosecution witness list. Every prosecution witness is an opportunity — experienced cross-examination exposes inconsistencies and unreliable testimony.
  • Challenge forensic and digital evidence on chain of custody, methodology, and accuracy grounds.

The trial is where criminal cases are won or lost. A skilled criminal defence lawyer's strategy includes: structured cross-examination challenging the credibility of each prosecution witness; highlighting contradictions between statements at different stages; challenging admissibility of evidence from unlawful searches or coerced confessions; presenting a clear defence narrative; and submitting final written arguments citing High Court and Appellate Division precedents.

Advocate Md. Shah Alam has extensive trial experience across all major criminal case categories before Magistrate Courts, Sessions Courts, and the High Court Division.

If a conviction occurs at trial court level, appeal rights remain:

  • Magistrate Court conviction: Appeal to Sessions Court, then High Court Division
  • Sessions Court conviction: Appeal directly to High Court Division
  • High Court Division: Appeal to Appellate Division (requires leave)

Common grounds include: improper evaluation of evidence, wrong legal standard applied, procedural irregularities, and new evidence. An experienced Supreme Court lawyer can identify and pursue appellate grounds aggressively.

sections.h2: Common Criminal Defence Mistakes to Avoid
sections.content:
1. Delaying hiring a lawyer. Call immediately — bail windows close fast and investigation solidifies quickly.
  2. Speaking to police without legal advice. Say nothing until your lawyer is present.
  3. Missing court hearing dates. This results in a warrant and possible bail cancellation. Attend every hearing.
  4. Informal settlements without legal advice. Paying money to a complainant does not legally close the case.
  5. Choosing a lawyer based on cost alone. In criminal law, lawyer quality directly determines your outcome.

Contact Advocate Md. Shah Alam for emergency criminal defence in Dhaka — available 7 days a week, same-day bail filing for urgent cases.

faqs.question: What is the best defence strategy for a criminal case in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: The best strategy depends on the specific charge. Generally: secure bail immediately, obtain and analyse the FIR, identify grounds to quash the FIR or challenge charge framing, build a factual and witness-based defence, and prepare thorough cross-examination. A criminal lawyer must be engaged from day one.

faqs.question: Can I be acquitted if the prosecution's evidence is weak in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Yes. Bangladesh courts apply the 'beyond reasonable doubt' standard. If the prosecution cannot prove guilt to this standard, the accused must be acquitted. A skilled lawyer creates and exploits doubt through cross-examination, evidence challenges, and legal argument.

faqs.question: How do I challenge a false FIR in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: File an application to quash the FIR in the High Court Division under Section 561A CrPC or via writ jurisdiction under Article 102. Courts quash FIRs that are manifestly false, vague and omnibus, or filed with malicious intent. Apply for anticipatory bail simultaneously.

faqs.question: What happens at a charge framing hearing in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Charge framing is the stage where the court decides if there is sufficient prima facie evidence to proceed to trial. Your lawyer can argue the chargesheet is defective, evidence is insufficient, or the accused should be discharged — ending the case without a full trial.

faqs.question: Can my criminal conviction be overturned on appeal in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Yes. Common grounds include: insufficient evidence, errors in applying legal standards, procedural irregularities, improper exclusion of defence evidence, and unreliable witness testimony. File the appeal within the limitation period — typically 30–60 days from the conviction order.
